items.title: Belgrade: Over 100,000 people join anti-gov't protests in one of Serbia's largest rallies to date
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tens of thousands of protesters from all over Serbia have descended upon the capital Belgrade as the government struggles to stem the monthslong outrage over the deadly collapse of a train station roof in November

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Thousands of people gather in Belgrade to protest widespread government corruption
1:34 Olivera Zivkovic, DW Reporter in Belgrade, on the protesters grievances
4:38 Idro Seferi, DW Correspondent in Belgrade, on student protesters holding 15 minutes of silence for those killed in the Novi Sad train station incident

items.title: Orban accuses the EU of abusing its power in fiery speech commemorating Hungary’s Independence Day
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>On Saturday, Viktor Orban held a speech at an Independence Day rally commemorating Hungary's revolution against the Austrian Empire on March 15th, 1848. Orban accused the EU of "abusing" its authority over Hungary, comparing it to how the Austrian Empire once controlled Hungary under its reign. He also labelled journalists and foreign-funded NGOs "bugs". Hungary is a member of the European Union, yet is at odds with some of the EU's stated policy positions.

DW's Senior International Correspondent Fanny Facsar reports from Budapest. 
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: UK's PM Starmer calls on Putin to accept Trump's proposed Ukraine ceasefire | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has held a virtual summit to discuss military aid for Ukraine, as well as a peacekeeping force led by a 'coalition of the willing' to be stationed in Ukraine in the event of a ceasefire. 

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 UK’s Starmer tells global leaders to "keep pressure" on Putin over ceasefire in Ukraine
1:22 Domitilla Sagramoso, Senior Lecturer in Security and Development at King's College London, on Starmer's efforts to lead a 'coalition of the willing' to make up for possible security gaps left by the United States

items.title: Africa's battle for new blood givers | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>How to tackle Africa's blood donation crisis? We look at efforts in Nigeria to get more people to give blood as health services struggle to find enough volunteer donors to meet demand. We hear from a hematologist on the front line of the country's battle for new blood givers who says cultural myths around donation are holding people back.

items.title: Will Friedrich Merz get along with the European Commission? | Berlin Briefing Podcast
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Germany’s likely next Chancellor Friedrich Merz is already hailed as the man who could forge what the European Union needs most: Unity. So far, Merz has seen a lot of advance praise, but how will his ambition to turn Germany from a “hesitant” to a “leading middle power” really be felt in Europe. How will he shape German relations with other EU countries? Will he get along with the European Commission?

Andreas Rinke, Chief Correspondent at Reuters, talks to DW's Chief Political Editor Michaela Küfner in the latest #BerlinBriefing podcast.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Poland's nuclear shift — back to the future? | Focus on Europe
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Poland is planning on replacing climate-damaging coal with nuclear power. Three American-style reactors are to be built directly on the Baltic Sea. According to surveys, around 90 percent of Poles support their country going nuclear.

items.title: Mexicans searching for missing relatives uncover site of an apparent mass killing | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Mexico's federal government has taken over an investigation after a group of civilians searching for missing relatives uncovered a site of an apparent mass killing. The discovery has raised questions over failures of police to uncover evidence at the same location. The site is most likely linked to Mexico's violent drug gangs. 

Chapters:
00:00 Possible mass killing site discovered in Mexico
02:35 Jules Giessler, DW Reporter

items.title: Putin sets out tough conditions for Ukraine ceasefire | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Trump's special envoy Steve Witkoff is in Moscow to discuss the US peace plan with Vladimir Putin. Washington is pushing for a quick end to the war — now in its fourth year. Putin has said he agreed "in principle" with a US-backed proposal for a 30-day ceasefire in the Ukraine war, but stressed that "serious questions" remain. Kyiv has called Putin's comments "manipulative."

Chapters:
00:00 Zelenskyy claims Russia preparing to reject ceasefire proposal
02:30 Mikhail Alexseev, San Diego State University
06:13 Susan Stewart, Ukraine & Russia expert

items.title: Canada's new Prime Minister Carney vows to stand up to Trump | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>The former head of the Canadian central bank, Mark Carney, has been sworn in as Canada's new prime minister. Carney, who replaces long-time leader Justin Trudeau, is considered crisis-tested thanks to his extensive national and international experience. With Canada facing sweeping US tariffs, the 59-year-old said he hopes to find ways to work together with the Trump administration.

00:00 Mark Carney sworn in as 24th prime minister
01:42 Lucy Van Oldenbarneveld, Journalist and Jeffrey Frankel, Harvard University
11:54 Lydia Miljan, University of Windsor
14:53 Daniel Beland, McGill Institute for the Study of Canada


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: G7 ministers voice 'unwaivering support' for Ukraine | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Foreign ministers from the G7 nations have reaffirmed their unwaivering support for Ukraine and its territorial integrity. Speaking after the summit, US Secretary of State Marco Rubio echoed the Kremlin's comments, saying he was "cautiously optimistic" about a ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia.

items.title: US cuts in science and research spark fears of far-reaching damage | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Unprecedented cuts to federal programs in the United States have hit everything from international aid to veterans' benefits. One area that Donald Trump and his appointees have been particularly keen to cut has been scientific research. That's sparked fears of wide-ranging harm to healthcare, climate protection and food safety.

Chapters:
00:00 US cuts in science and research spark fears of far-reaching damage

09:50 Julie McNamara, Union of Concerned Scientists

items.title: Philippines ex-President Duterte attends ICC hearing virtually, charged for notorious 'war on drugs'
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Rodrigo Duterte, the former Philippine President, has appeared at the International Criminal Court in the Hague. He's facing charges of crimes against humanity for his deadly crackdown on drugs. The 79-year old appeared via video link from a detention center. Duterte's lawyer says was unable to contribute to his hearing due to debilitating medical issues. But the judge said a court doctor found Duterte to be fully mentally aware and fit. A pre-trial hearing date has been set for September. Duterte was arrested in Manila this week and flown to the Netherlands. 

#icc #duterte 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: US and China going eye to eye on tariffs | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>So far in 2025, the US and China are going punch for punch when it comes to tariffs on each other's exports. But how much hurt can both economies take? In particular, the Chinese domestic economy has failed to launch and now the country's leaders have unveiled some plans to boost domestic demand. But as whiplash tariffs from the American side leave that country's trading partners bewildered, we're wondering how much pain are both sides willing to tolerate?

#tariffs #dwbusiness #china 
For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Too hard to swallow? Why Trump's proposed tariffs on European wine could crush the EU wine industry
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump has threatened to impose 200% tariffs on European wine. This comes after the EU retaliated against US duties on European steel and aluminum. As a top wine producer, France is particularly vulnerable to the latest levies.

Chapter Breakdown
0:00 Trump threatens 200% tariffs on EU wine
1:34 Marie Sina, DW Business, on the escalating tariff 'ping pong'
5:27 Ernst Büscher, German Wine Institute Spokesperson, on how important the US market is for German and French winemakers
